elevator_pitch: Go-Nuts is a Crumble-style gourmet donut company with a rotating weekly menu of six oversized, creative donut flavors. Customers check the menu each week, order online, and get hot, fresh donuts delivered to their door, even late at night.
problem: Traditional donut shops serve the same stale flavors every day, close early, and never deliver. There is no premium, experience-driven donut brand that creates weekly anticipation the way Crumble Cookies does for cookies.
solution: Go-Nuts offers six oversized gourmet donuts on a rotating weekly menu, combining two classic staples with four bold, limited-edition flavors. Orders are delivered hot and fresh, with availability extending into late-night hours when cravings hit hardest.
target_market: Millennials and Gen Z consumers aged 18 to 35 who follow food trends, order delivery frequently, and enjoy the 'drop culture' of rotating limited-edition products. Secondary market includes families and office catering.
revenue_model: Revenue comes from direct-to-consumer donut sales via storefront and delivery, with premium pricing on oversized gourmet donuts (averaging $5 to $6 per donut, or $28 to $34 per six-pack). Additional revenue from branded merchandise, corporate gifting boxes, and catering packages.
key_features: Rotating weekly menu with six oversized gourmet flavors
Mix of cake and yeast donuts for variety
Weekly flavor teasers and social media drops to build anticipation
competitive_advantage: Go-Nuts combines the scarcity-driven rotating menu model (proven by Crumble Cookies) with the underserved gourmet donut market. Unlike Krispy Kreme or local shops, Go-Nuts creates a weekly event around its menu, driving repeat visits and social media buzz. Late-night delivery fills a gap no major donut brand currently owns.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. donut market is valued at roughly $20 billion annually. The premium/gourmet segment is growing at 8% per year. Even capturing 0.1% of the national market represents $20 million in annual revenue. In a single metro area, a Go-Nuts location could reasonably target $1.5 to $3 million in year-one revenue.
go_to_market: Launch with a single flagship storefront in a college town or trendy urban neighborhood. Build hype pre-launch with weekly flavor reveals on Instagram and TikTok. Offer free delivery within a 5-mile radius for the first month to build the customer base and collect reviews.
risks: Donut freshness degrades quickly during delivery, requiring tight logistics
Rotating menus demand constant R&D and risk flavor fatigue or misses
Low barriers to entry mean local bakeries or larger chains could copy the model
year_one_milestones: Open flagship location and achieve $150K in monthly revenue by month 6
Build an Instagram and TikTok following of 50,000 combined followers
Launch a second location or licensed delivery kitchen in an adjacent market
